title: Fear No More – Concept Mastery Helper
author: William Shakespeare

concepts.lesson: The poem says that after death a person no longer suffers from heat, cold, or hardships.
concepts.real_example: Many literary works describe death as a release from pain.

concepts.lesson: The poem shows that both rich and poor meet the same fate in death.
concepts.real_example: Regardless of social status, everyone eventually dies.

concepts.lesson: Death frees a person from fear of criticism, slander, and other worries.
concepts.real_example: People often worry about social judgement during life.

concepts.lesson: The poem emphasises that death is inevitable and universal for all humans.
concepts.real_example: Across cultures and history, death is recognised as a universal human reality.
